response: The global food system faces unprecedented pressure due to population growth (projected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050), requiring a 60% increase in food production, while climate change reduces crop yields and agriculture contributes 25% of greenhouse gas emissions. Water scarcity and soil degradation—affecting 33% of arable land—further strain production, compounded by food waste (one-third of all food lost). Potential solutions include precision agriculture, vertical farming, alternative proteins, genetic modification, and regenerative practices, alongside policy reforms. However, scaling these solutions while keeping food affordable and accessible remains a critical challenge.
